title: Computer Architecture & Systems — RISC-V Labs
summary: A multi-unit course benchmark spanning C programming, RISC-V assembly, SIMD intrinsics, OpenMP parallelism, image processing, and a matrix computation library. Labs are mostly independent; Project 1 has an imageloader → steganography → gameoflife chain, and Project 4 has a memory-primitives → arithmetic/multiply → exponentiation chain.
instructionPreview: This task comes from an undergraduate computer-architecture and systems sequence. It spans multiple labs and projects covering C programming, RISC-V assembly, SIMD intrinsics, OpenMP parallelism, and a matrix computation library.
instruction:
This task comes from an undergraduate computer-architecture and systems sequence. It spans
multiple labs and projects covering C programming, RISC-V assembly, SIMD intrinsics,
OpenMP parallelism, and a matrix computation library.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is already present.
You need to implement all the modules and features listed below so that
all tests pass.

## Modules to Implement

- **Lab01 — Linked List Cycle Detection** (`Lab/lab01/`): Implement cycle detection
  in a singly-linked list. The function receives a pointer to the head node and must
  determine whether the list contains a cycle. (~1 file, ~1 function to implement)

- **Lab02 — Bit Operations & LFSR** (`Lab/lab02/`): Implement low-level bit extraction,
  setting, and flipping operations on unsigned integers. Also implement a 16-bit Galois
  linear feedback shift register that cycles through all 65535 non-zero values.
  Build using `make bit_ops lfsr` in `Lab/lab02/`. (~2 files, ~4 functions to implement)

- **Lab02 — Vector Data Structure** (`Lab/lab02/`): Complete the dynamic vector
  implementation including memory allocation, element access, deallocation, and
  resizing. Fill in the `YOUR CODE HERE` placeholders in both `vector.c` and
  `vector.h`. Build using `make vector-test` in `Lab/lab02/`.
  (~2 files, ~4 functions to implement)

- **Lab03 — RISC-V Assembly** (`Lab/lab03/`): Implement a recursive factorial function
  in RISC-V assembly, a linked-list map function that applies a function pointer to
  each node, and fix calling convention violations in a test program. The VENUS
  simulator jar is available at `Lab/tools/venus.jar`.
  (~3 files, ~4 functions to implement)

- **Lab04 — RISC-V Discrete Function** (`Lab/lab04/`): Implement a function in RISC-V
  assembly that looks up values from a precomputed array given an integer input.
  (~1 file, ~1 function to implement)

- **Lab07 — Cache-Optimized Transpose** (`Lab/lab07/`): Implement a cache-blocked
  matrix transpose that works for matrices whose dimensions are not necessarily
  multiples of the block size. Build using `make` in `Lab/lab07/`.
  (~1 file, ~1 function to implement)

- **Lab09 — SIMD Operations** (`Lab/lab09/`): Implement SSE-based SIMD versions of
  a conditional sum operation (summing elements >= 128), including both a basic SIMD
  version and an unrolled SIMD version. Build using `make` in `Lab/lab09/`.
  (~1 file, ~2 functions to implement)

- **Lab10 — OpenMP Parallelism** (`Lab/lab10/`): Optimize parallel vector addition
  using both adjacent-element and chunk-based work distribution without using OpenMP
  `for` directives. Also optimize a dot product using manual reduction (without the
  `reduction` directive) and using the `reduction` directive. Build using `make` in
  `Lab/lab10/`. (~1 file, ~4 functions to implement)

- **Project 1 — Image Processing** (`Project/proj1/`): Implement PPM P3 image file
  I/O (read, write, free), steganography via LSB extraction of the blue channel,
  and Conway's Game of Life on colour images with configurable birth/survival rules
  applied independently per RGB channel on a toroidal grid. Build with `make all`
  in `Project/proj1/`. (~3 files, ~7 functions to implement)

- **Project 4 — Matrix Library** (`Project/proj4/`): Implement a matrix computation
  library including memory allocation (with reference counting for sliced matrices),
  element access, fill, element-wise arithmetic (add, subtract, negate, absolute
  value), matrix multiplication, and matrix exponentiation via repeated squaring.
  (~1 file, ~12 functions to implement)

## Test-Coupled Signatures

The following functions are called directly by the test suite. You must implement
them with these exact signatures:

- `int ll_has_cycle(node *head)` — returns 1 if the linked list has a cycle, 0 otherwise
- `unsigned get_bit(unsigned x, unsigned n)` — returns the nth bit of x
- `void set_bit(unsigned *x, unsigned n, unsigned v)` — sets the nth bit of *x to v
- `void flip_bit(unsigned *x, unsigned n)` — toggles the nth bit of *x
- `void lfsr_calculate(uint16_t *reg)` — advances a 16-bit Galois LFSR one step
- `vector_t *vector_new()` — allocates a new vector of size 1 initialized to 0
- `int vector_get(vector_t *v, size_t loc)` — returns the value at index loc
- `void vector_delete(vector_t *v)` — frees all memory for the vector
- `void vector_set(vector_t *v, size_t loc, int value)` — sets value at loc, growing if needed
- `void transpose_blocking(int n, int blocksize, int *dst, int *src)` — cache-blocked transpose
- `long long int sum_simd(int vals[NUM_ELEMS])` — SIMD conditional sum of elements >= 128
- `long long int sum_simd_unrolled(int vals[NUM_ELEMS])` — unrolled SIMD conditional sum
- `void v_add_optimized_adjacent(double *x, double *y, double *z)` — parallel vector add (adjacent)
- `void v_add_optimized_chunks(double *x, double *y, double *z)` — parallel vector add (chunks)
- `double dotp_manual_optimized(double *x, double *y, int arr_size)` — manual reduction dot product
- `double dotp_reduction_optimized(double *x, double *y, int arr_size)` — OpenMP reduction dot product
- `Image *readData(char *filename)` — parses a P3 PPM file into an Image struct
- `void writeData(Image *image)` — prints the image to stdout in P3 PPM format
- `void freeImage(Image *image)` — frees all allocated memory for an image
- `Color *evaluateOnePixel(Image *image, int row, int col)` — LSB steganography extraction
- `Image *steganography(Image *image)` — applies evaluateOnePixel to create output image
- `Color *evaluateOneCell(Image *image, int row, int col, uint32_t rule)` — Game of Life cell update
- `Image *life(Image *image, uint32_t rule)` — one iteration of the Game of Life
- `int allocate_matrix(matrix **mat, int rows, int cols)` — allocates a zero-initialized matrix
- `int allocate_matrix_ref(matrix **mat, matrix *from, int row_offset, int col_offset, int rows, int cols)` — creates a matrix slice
- `void deallocate_matrix(matrix *mat)` — frees matrix memory (respects reference counts)
- `double get(matrix *mat, int row, int col)` — returns element at [row, col]
- `void set(matrix *mat, int row, int col, double val)` — sets element at [row, col]
- `void fill_matrix(matrix *mat, double val)` — fills all entries with val
- `int add_matrix(matrix *result, matrix *mat1, matrix *mat2)` — element-wise addition
- `int sub_matrix(matrix *result, matrix *mat1, matrix *mat2)` — element-wise subtraction
- `int mul_matrix(matrix *result, matrix *mat1, matrix *mat2)` — matrix multiplication
- `int pow_matrix(matrix *result, matrix *mat, int pow)` — matrix exponentiation
- `int neg_matrix(matrix *result, matrix *mat)` — element-wise negation
- `int abs_matrix(matrix *result, matrix *mat)` — element-wise absolute value

moduleDag.description: A multi-unit course benchmark spanning C programming, RISC-V assembly, SIMD intrinsics, OpenMP parallelism, image processing, and a matrix computation library. Labs are mostly independent; Project 1 has an imageloader → steganography → gameoflife chain, and Project 4 has a memory-primitives → arithmetic/multiply → exponentiation chain.

nodes.label: Lab01 — Linked List Cycle Detection
nodes.description: Implements Floyd's tortoise-and-hare cycle detection on a singly-linked list; returns 1 if a cycle is present, 0 otherwise.

nodes.label: Lab02 — Bit Operations & Galois LFSR
nodes.description: Implements bit extraction, setting, and flipping on unsigned 32-bit integers (get_bit, set_bit, flip_bit) and a 16-bit Galois LFSR that advances one step per call, cycling through all 65535 non-zero states.

nodes.label: Lab02 — Dynamic Vector
nodes.description: A heap-allocated resizable integer array supporting construction, element get/set with automatic capacity growth, and full deallocation. Implements vector.c and the missing declarations in vector.h.

nodes.label: Lab03 — RISC-V Assembly
nodes.description: Three RISC-V assembly programs: recursive factorial (factorial.s), linked-list map with a function-pointer callback (list_map.s), and a calling-convention bug-fix exercise that repairs prologue/epilogue register saves in three functions (cc_test.s).

nodes.label: Lab04 — RISC-V Discrete Function Lookup
nodes.description: A RISC-V assembly routine that maps an integer input to an output array entry using index arithmetic and a word-size load. Requires calling- convention knowledge (prologue/epilogue, saved vs. temp registers) established in lab03.

nodes.label: Lab07 — Cache-Blocked Matrix Transpose
nodes.description: A cache-optimised square-matrix transpose tiled into configurable blocks; correctly handles matrices whose dimension is not a multiple of the block size by clamping inner loop bounds.

nodes.label: Lab09 — SIMD Conditional Sum
nodes.description: Two SSE-vectorised functions that accumulate array elements >= 128: a basic 4-wide loop (sum_simd) and a 4x-unrolled variant (sum_simd_unrolled) for higher throughput; both include scalar tail cases for remaining elements.

nodes.label: Lab10 — OpenMP Parallelism
nodes.description: Four OpenMP routines: adjacent-stride and chunk-partitioned parallel vector addition (without the `for` directive), and manual per-thread accumulation and `reduction`-clause dot products.

nodes.label: Project 1 — PPM Image I/O
nodes.description: Foundational image layer: parses a P3 PPM file into a heap-allocated Image struct (readData), serialises an Image to P3 stdout (writeData), and frees all associated memory (freeImage). All other proj1 modules depend on this Image representation.

nodes.label: Project 1 — LSB Steganography
nodes.description: Extracts a hidden image from the LSB of each pixel's blue channel: evaluateOnePixel maps one pixel to white/black; steganography applies it across the whole image and returns a new Image. Depends on the Image struct and allocation pattern from proj1_imageio.

nodes.label: Project 1 — Conway's Game of Life
nodes.description: Rule-parameterised Game of Life on a toroidal colour grid, processing each RGB channel independently (evaluateOneCell) with a full-frame driver (life). Mirrors the per-pixel driver pattern from steganography.c, which the task spec explicitly says to copy from.

nodes.label: Project 4 — Matrix Memory & Basic Access
nodes.description: Foundation of the matrix library: contiguous zero-initialised allocation (allocate_matrix), reference-counted slice creation (allocate_matrix_ref), ref-count-aware deallocation (deallocate_matrix), and trivial element accessors get, set, and fill_matrix. Everything else in proj4 depends on this.

nodes.label: Project 4 — Element-wise Arithmetic
nodes.description: Element-wise matrix operations (add_matrix, sub_matrix, neg_matrix, abs_matrix) implemented as nested loops over mat->data[i][j], relying on the element access primitives from proj4_memory.

nodes.label: Project 4 — Matrix Multiplication
nodes.description: Standard O(n³) matrix multiplication (mul_matrix) using nested loops and direct element access from proj4_memory. Must be implemented before proj4_pow, which calls mul_matrix in its squaring loop.

nodes.label: Project 4 — Matrix Exponentiation
nodes.description: Matrix exponentiation by repeated squaring (pow_matrix): initialises result to identity, halves the exponent while squaring the base matrix, multiplying result when the low bit is set. Calls mul_matrix, allocate_ matrix, and deallocate_matrix, so proj4_memory and proj4_multiply must both be complete.

edges.label: lab04 requires RISC-V calling-convention knowledge (prologue/epilogue, saved vs. temp registers) established in lab03
steganography.c operates on the Image struct allocated and freed by imageloader.c
gameoflife.c uses the same Image allocation and I/O layer defined in imageloader.c
task spec says to copy the per-pixel driver from steganography.c; gameoflife uses the same evaluateOnePixel→driver pattern
add/sub/neg/abs all read and write elements via mat->data[i][j] established in proj4_memory
mul_matrix reads elements via mat->data[i][k] and mat->data[k][j] defined in proj4_memory
pow_matrix calls mul_matrix directly inside its repeated-squaring loop
